findings.description: Comprehensive GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline with 5 workflow files covering tests, pre-commit checks, publishing, dependency locking, and security scanning
findings.evidence: Workflows found in .github/workflows/: tests.yaml (comprehensive test matrix across Python 3.10-3.14, PyPy 3.11, Windows, macOS, Linux with 11 environments total), pre-commit.yaml, publish.yaml, lock.yaml, zizmor.yaml. Test workflow uses modern tooling: astral-sh/setup-uv@v8.0.0, actions pinned to commit SHAs (actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd), concurrency controls for canceling redundant runs.
findings.recommendation: Maintain current CI/CD practices. Consider adding periodic dependency vulnerability scanning if not already covered by zizmor.yaml.

findings.description: Advanced testing strategy includes minimum and development version testing to ensure compatibility across dependency version ranges
findings.evidence: pyproject.toml shows tox environments: tests-min (Python 3.14 with pinned minimum versions: blinker==1.9.0, click==8.1.3, werkzeug==3.1.0), tests-dev (Python 3.10 with git main branches of all Pallets dependencies). tests.yaml matrix includes: {name: Minimum Versions, python: '3.14', tox: tests-min}, {name: Development Versions, python: '3.10', tox: tests-dev}
findings.recommendation: Continue this excellent practice of boundary testing. This catches both backward compatibility issues and upcoming breaking changes.

findings.description: Zero TODO/FIXME markers found in codebase, indicating excellent code hygiene and minimal technical debt
findings.evidence: Code search query 'repo:pallets/flask TODO OR FIXME language:python' returned total_count: 0, incomplete_results: false
findings.recommendation: Maintain current code quality standards. Continue using pre-commit hooks and code review to prevent accumulation of technical debt markers.

findings.description: Strict type checking and linting enforcement with multiple quality tools configured
findings.evidence: pyproject.toml shows: [tool.mypy] strict = true, python_version = '3.10', [tool.pyright] typeCheckingMode = 'basic', [tool.ruff.lint] select = ['B', 'E', 'F', 'I', 'UP', 'W'] (flake8-bugbear, pycodestyle, pyflakes, isort, pyupgrade). Pre-commit configuration present: .pre-commit-config.yaml (size: 827 bytes). Pytest configured with filterwarnings = ['error'] treating warnings as errors.
findings.recommendation: Consider upgrading Pyright to 'strict' mode to match MyPy strictness level for even more comprehensive type coverage.

findings.description: Modern Python project structure following best practices with src-layout, comprehensive testing, and documentation
findings.evidence: Repository tree shows: src/ directory (src-layout), tests/ directory, docs/ directory, examples/ directory, pyproject.toml (modern PEP 517/518 packaging), uv.lock (310103 bytes), .readthedocs.yaml, .devcontainer/, .editorconfig, CHANGES.rst (73377 bytes changelog). Build system: requires = ['flit_core>=3.11,<4'], build-backend = 'flit_core.buildapi'
findings.recommendation: Continue following modern Python packaging standards. The src-layout and comprehensive documentation are exemplary.

findings.description: State-of-the-art dependency management with lock file, dependency groups, and automated update workflows
findings.evidence: uv.lock file present (310103 bytes). pyproject.toml uses dependency-groups: dev (ruff, tox, tox-uv), tests (pytest, asgiref, greenlet), typing (mypy, pyright, type stubs), docs (sphinx, pallets-sphinx-themes), pre-commit. Runtime dependencies pinned to minimums: werkzeug>=3.1.0, jinja2>=3.1.2. Workflow .github/workflows/lock.yaml for automated dependency lock updates.
findings.recommendation: Excellent dependency management. Continue using uv for fast, reproducible builds and maintain the lock file update workflow.

findings.description: Repository is actively maintained with very recent commits from core maintainers
findings.evidence: Recent commits: '2ac89889f4cc330eabd50f295dcef02828522c69' (2026-04-09T04:04:03Z, davidism: 'Merge branch stable'), '689362089edd09b6d68f7cfe99075e1345e0fede' (2026-04-09T04:01:29Z, davidism: 'fix typo'), '258d68b6ff5e2244386540f48b48bab90d6ab827' (2026-04-05T19:32:14Z, davidism: 'Merge branch stable'). All 10 recent commits from April 2026 and March 2026.
findings.recommendation: Continue current maintenance velocity. The frequent merges from stable branch show good release management.

findings.description: Two pull requests have been open for over 12 months, though both are deliberate architectural refactorings by maintainers
findings.evidence: Open PRs: #5665 (pgjones, created 2025-01-02T21:10:36Z, updated 2025-03-29T22:19:39Z, 'Workflow', 15 months old), #5660 (pgjones, created 2024-12-24T20:09:08Z, updated 2026-03-31T06:13:29Z, 'Move parts of the session code to sansio', 15 months old), #5918 (davidism, created 2026-02-12T22:03:40Z, updated 2026-04-12T12:21:57Z, 'automatic options as separate route', 2 months old, milestone: 3.2.0)
findings.recommendation: Consider converting long-running PRs #5665 and #5660 to draft status if they are experimental, or create tracking issues to document the architectural work separately from the implementation PR. PR #5918 is appropriately tracked with milestone 3.2.0.

findings.description: Comprehensive test configuration with branch coverage tracking and strict warning treatment
findings.evidence: pyproject.toml shows: [tool.pytest.ini_options] testpaths = ['tests'], filterwarnings = ['error']. [tool.coverage.run] branch = true, source = ['flask', 'tests']. [tool.coverage.paths] source = ['src', '*/site-packages']. [tool.coverage.report] exclude_also patterns for TYPE_CHECKING and NotImplementedError blocks.
findings.recommendation: Maintain strict pytest configuration. The warnings-as-errors policy ensures high code quality and prevents warning accumulation.

findings.description: Professional documentation setup with ReadTheDocs integration, comprehensive changelog, and examples
findings.evidence: Repository contains: docs/ directory, .readthedocs.yaml (242 bytes), CHANGES.rst (73377 bytes detailed changelog), examples/ directory, README.md (1639 bytes). pyproject.toml dependency-groups includes docs: ['pallets-sphinx-themes', 'sphinx<9', 'sphinx-tabs', 'sphinxcontrib-log-cabinet'] and docs-auto for live rebuilding.
findings.recommendation: Excellent documentation infrastructure. Continue maintaining the detailed CHANGES.rst for version tracking and providing examples for users.

findings.description: Severe bus factor risk - 100% of recent commits by single maintainer. All 10 most recent commits (from 2026-03-08 to 2026-04-09) were authored by davidism (David Lord). No other contributors visible in recent activity, creating a single point of failure for this critical web framework.
findings.evidence: list_commits returned 10 commits, all by davidism: '2ac89889f4cc330eabd50f295dcef02828522c69' (2026-04-09 Merge branch stable), '689362089edd09b6d68f7cfe99075e1345e0fede' (2026-04-09 fix typo), '258d68b6ff5e2244386540f48b48bab90d6ab827' (2026-04-05 Merge branch stable), 'a31e6b73469cb2bf7eb8f70b5ff21f710fd2e23c' (2026-04-05 remove werkzeug host tests), 'e4e4bf6543ac1f132afddb1ffd0bf02bea4c93f7' (2026-04-05 Merge branch stable), 'b21425d6df207fec0c47e9563faa10a2819984ca' (2026-04-05 reduce venv size), '83dbcb222a65a87741c9d96bb183f34528149269' (2026-04-03 update dev dependencies), '7ef2946fb5151b745df30201b8c27790cac53875' (2026-03-24 remove unicode host test), '91c6b3fecf36b1f04554e57cc4060ccb737a445d' (2026-03-24 remove unicode host test), '4cae5d8e411b1e69949d8fae669afeacbd3e5908' (2026-03-08 Merge branch stable). Author field for all: davidism (login), ID 1242887.
findings.recommendation: Urgently recruit 2-3 co-maintainers to distribute maintenance burden. Document succession plan and create MAINTAINERS file. Consider establishing a governance model that encourages broader contributor participation. Pallets organization should prioritize maintainer diversity for this critical project.

findings.description: Missing critical governance documentation in repository. No CONTRIBUTING.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, or GOVERNANCE.md files found in root directory, which may discourage new contributors and create ambiguity around contribution process and community standards.
findings.evidence: get_repository_tree returned 15 items in root: '.devcontainer', '.editorconfig', '.github', '.gitignore', '.pre-commit-config.yaml', '.readthedocs.yaml', 'CHANGES.rst', 'LICENSE.txt', 'README.md', 'docs', 'examples', 'pyproject.toml', 'src', 'tests', 'uv.lock'. No CONTRIBUTING.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, or GOVERNANCE.md files present.
findings.recommendation: Create CONTRIBUTING.md with clear guidelines for issue reporting, pull requests, testing requirements, and code style. Add CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md to establish community standards (or link to Pallets org-wide policy). Document governance structure in MAINTAINERS or GOVERNANCE.md file.

findings.description: Zero open issues may indicate overly aggressive closure policy or gatekeeping that could discourage community feature proposals and legitimate bug reports. While rapid triage is positive, having no open issues for a project of this size is unusual and may signal barriers to contribution.
findings.evidence: list_issues with state=OPEN returned: 'issues':[], 'totalCount':0. list_issues with state=CLOSED returned: 'totalCount':2735, with recent issues showing very rapid closure times (e.g., #5988 closed 2 minutes after opening, #5991 closed 8 minutes after opening, #6000-6002 duplicates closed within hours as spam).
findings.recommendation: Evaluate issue closure policy to ensure legitimate feature requests and non-trivial bugs remain open for community discussion. Consider keeping a backlog of accepted feature proposals labeled as 'enhancement' or 'help wanted'. Create 'good first issue' labels to encourage new contributors.

findings.description: Excellent issue triage infrastructure with templates for bug reports and feature requests. Issues are triaged rapidly (minutes to hours for simple issues, days for complex security issues). 2,735 closed issues demonstrate long history of active maintenance.
findings.evidence: get_file_contents for .github returned: 'ISSUE_TEMPLATE' directory containing 'bug-report.md', 'feature-request.md', and 'config.yml'. search_code confirmed 3 template files exist. Recent closed issues show rapid response: #5988 (typo) opened 2026-04-09 03:59, closed 04:01 (2 minutes, 2 comments); #5991 (security) opened 2026-04-10 16:10, closed 16:18 (8 minutes, 2 comments); #5965 (bug) opened 2026-03-27, closed 2026-04-11 (15 days, 1 comment).
findings.recommendation: Maintain current issue template quality. Consider adding issue triage labels to help community members understand status. Document expected response times for different issue types in CONTRIBUTING.md.

findings.description: Highly responsive maintainer engagement with rapid response times for legitimate issues. Simple issues resolved in minutes, complex security issues handled appropriately within days-weeks. All issues receive at least one comment before closure, showing active engagement.
findings.evidence: Recent closed issues from list_issues show: #5988 (docs typo) - 2 minutes response time with 2 comments, #5991 (13 CVEs) - 8 minutes response time with 2 comments, #5965 (celery bug) - 15 days with 1 comment, #5972 (vulnerability) - 15 days with 1 comment, #5993 (spam) - closed within hours with 1 comment. All issues show maintainer davidism actively commenting and closing.
findings.recommendation: Continue current engagement standards. To scale beyond single maintainer, consider empowering trusted community members as triagers who can label and respond to issues without full commit access.

findings.description: Exemplary release management with regular, well-documented releases following semantic versioning. Security patches released promptly, feature releases annually, comprehensive CHANGES.rst with 73,377 bytes of detailed changelog dating back to version 0.1 (2010). Currently developing version 3.2.0.
findings.evidence: get_file_contents for CHANGES.rst (SHA a5fa63f14e7260b762ff07ad7f097a2d9b92762f, 73,377 bytes) shows: Version 3.1.3 (2026-02-18) - security fix with GHSA reference, Version 3.1.2 (2025-08-19) - bug fixes, Version 3.1.1 (2025-05-13) - security fix ghsa:4grg-w6v8-c28g, Version 3.1.0 (2024-11-13) - feature release, Version 3.0.3 (2024-04-07), Version 3.0.2 (2024-02-03), Version 3.0.1 (2024-01-18), Version 3.0.0 (2023-09-30) - major release. Each version includes detailed PR/issue references and deprecation warnings.
findings.recommendation: Maintain current release documentation standards. Consider publishing release roadmap or milestones to give community visibility into upcoming features. Continue security-responsive patching.

findings.description: Professional development infrastructure including CI/CD workflows, pre-commit hooks, documentation hosting, and pull request templates. Repository shows mature engineering practices despite missing some governance documents.
findings.evidence: get_repository_tree shows: '.github/workflows' directory (CI/CD automation), '.pre-commit-config.yaml' (code quality checks), '.readthedocs.yaml' (documentation hosting), 'tests' directory (test suite), '.github/pull_request_template.md' (822 bytes), 'pyproject.toml' (6,557 bytes modern packaging), 'uv.lock' (310,103 bytes dependency lock), 'docs' directory, 'examples' directory.
findings.recommendation: Continue maintaining high engineering standards. Document development setup process in CONTRIBUTING.md to help new contributors leverage existing tooling.

findings.description: README.md provides a clear project introduction with branding, description, and working example code. It includes a minimal 'Hello World' example that demonstrates Flask's simplicity.
findings.evidence: File: README.md (1,639 bytes, SHA: 64f56cac4f9d60020a39b9c2f803b1846796cf6d). Contains project description, simple code example (from flask import Flask / app = Flask(__name__) / @app.route('/') / def hello(): return 'Hello, World!'), links to Werkzeug/Jinja dependencies, donation info, and contributing section pointing to https://palletsprojects.com/contributing/
findings.recommendation: Consider adding explicit installation command (pip install Flask) directly in README for better discoverability.

findings.description: Comprehensive Sphinx-based documentation infrastructure with extensive API reference and topic guides. Professional documentation hosted on ReadTheDocs.
findings.evidence: docs/ folder contains: api.rst (21,212 bytes - dedicated API reference), quickstart.rst (30,055 bytes), tutorial/ subdirectory, patterns/ subdirectory, and 30+ .rst files covering blueprints, CLI, config, templating, testing, etc. Sphinx configuration present: conf.py (3,386 bytes), Makefile (634 bytes). ReadTheDocs integration: .readthedocs.yaml (242 bytes).
findings.recommendation: Maintain current exemplary standards. Documentation is comprehensive and well-organized.

findings.description: Exceptionally detailed changelog covering 15+ years of project history with structured format, version numbers, release dates, and issue/PR references.
findings.evidence: CHANGES.rst (73,377 bytes, SHA: a5fa63f14e7260b762ff07ad7f097a2d9b92762f). Contains version history from 3.2.0 (Unreleased) back to Version 0.1 (Released 2010-04-16). Each entry includes release date, categorized changes, issue/PR references (e.g., :pr:`5730`, :issue:`5639`), and security advisories (e.g., :ghsa:`68rp-wp8r-4726`).
findings.recommendation: Continue current excellent practices. This is a model changelog for open-source projects.

findings.description: Contributing guide exists but delegates to external Pallets organization documentation rather than being self-contained in repository.
findings.evidence: docs/contributing.rst (274 bytes) redirects to https://palletsprojects.com/contributing/. README.md also references this URL: 'See our [detailed contributing documentation][contrib] for many ways to contribute, including reporting issues, requesting features, asking or answering questions, and making PRs.'
findings.recommendation: Consider adding a CONTRIBUTING.md stub in repository root that summarizes key contribution steps and links to full external guide for better discoverability by new contributors.

findings.description: Multiple example projects demonstrating different Flask use cases and integration patterns, complemented by extensive tutorial documentation.
findings.evidence: examples/ directory contains 3 subdirectories: examples/tutorial/ (complete tutorial app), examples/celery/ (Celery task queue integration), examples/javascript/ (frontend integration). Documentation includes docs/tutorial/ subdirectory, docs/patterns/ subdirectory for advanced patterns, and quickstart.rst (30,055 bytes) with inline examples.
findings.recommendation: Excellent examples coverage. Continue providing diverse, real-world integration examples.

findings.description: Professional documentation build system with Sphinx, ReadTheDocs integration, and comprehensive topic coverage across 30+ documentation files.
findings.evidence: Repository tree shows: docs/ folder with conf.py, Makefile, make.bat, index.rst (2,065 bytes), and specialized guides including blueprints.rst (12,559 bytes), config.rst (29,006 bytes), cli.rst (16,701 bytes), errorhandling.rst (18,373 bytes), web-security.rst (13,085 bytes), testing.rst (10,426 bytes), extensiondev.rst (12,918 bytes), and deploying/ subdirectory. .readthedocs.yaml confirms hosted documentation.
findings.recommendation: No changes needed. This represents industry best practices for Python project documentation.

findings.description: Active, long-term maintained project with consistent release cadence and meticulous version tracking.
findings.evidence: CHANGES.rst shows releases from Version 3.1.3 (Released 2026-02-18), 3.1.2 (Released 2025-08-19), continuing through multiple major versions back to Version 0.1 (Released 2010-04-16). Includes codenames for major releases (e.g., 'Punsch', 'Limoncello', 'Calvados', 'Grappa') and detailed deprecation notices.
findings.recommendation: Continue maintaining detailed release notes and version history as currently practiced.

mcp_params.query: repo:pallets/flask shell=True OR eval( OR exec( language:python
repo:pallets/flask security report path:README OR path:docs

findings.description: No SECURITY.md file exists in the repository. There is no documented vulnerability disclosure policy or security contact information for responsible disclosure.
findings.evidence: Attempted to retrieve SECURITY.md and .github/SECURITY.md - both paths do not exist. Search query 'repo:pallets/flask filename:SECURITY' returned only 'docs/web-security.rst' which contains application security guidance, not a vulnerability disclosure policy. README.md links to https://palletsprojects.com/contributing/ but does not explicitly mention security reporting procedures.
findings.recommendation: Add a .github/SECURITY.md file with: (1) security contact email (e.g., security@palletsprojects.com), (2) expected response time for security reports, (3) supported versions and security update policy, (4) optional PGP key for encrypted communication. Follow GitHub's security policy template.

findings.description: GitHub Actions security analysis (zizmor) is configured, but no Python-specific SAST tools (CodeQL, Bandit, Semgrep) are detected in CI workflows.
findings.evidence: File .github/workflows/zizmor.yaml contains: 'uses: zizmorcore/zizmor-action@71321a20a9ded102f6e9ce5718a2fcec2c4f70d8 # v0.5.2' with triggers on pull_request and push events for YAML files. Workflow analyzes GitHub Actions security but not Python source code. Search of .github/workflows path returned 5 workflow files: lock.yaml, zizmor.yaml, publish.yaml, pre-commit.yaml, tests.yaml - none appear to run Python SAST.
findings.recommendation: Integrate Python-specific static analysis tools into CI pipeline. Consider adding: (1) GitHub CodeQL for comprehensive security scanning, (2) Bandit for Python security issue detection, or (3) Semgrep with Python security rules. This would complement the existing zizmor workflow security analysis.

findings.description: No Dependabot or Renovate configuration detected for automated dependency vulnerability scanning and updates. However, a comprehensive lockfile (uv.lock) is present.
findings.evidence: Attempted to retrieve .github/dependabot.yml - file does not exist. Repository tree shows 'uv.lock' file present (310,103 bytes) at path uv.lock, SHA: fe9f6cfa878635bea3675f02bb9ac6014ef498af. The lockfile indicates dependency pinning and reproducible builds, but no public configuration for automated dependency updates was found.
findings.recommendation: Configure Dependabot by adding .github/dependabot.yml to enable automated security updates for Python dependencies. This will provide automated pull requests for known vulnerabilities in dependencies. Alternatively, configure Renovate for more advanced dependency update management. The existing uv.lock should be maintained for reproducible builds.

findings.description: No dangerous code patterns detected in the codebase. Search for common Python security anti-patterns returned zero results.
findings.evidence: Code search query 'repo:pallets/flask shell=True OR eval( OR exec( language:python' returned: total_count: 0, incomplete_results: false. This indicates the codebase does not contain obvious unsafe patterns like subprocess calls with shell=True, or dynamic code execution via eval() or exec().
findings.recommendation: Maintain current code quality standards. Continue avoiding dangerous patterns like shell=True in subprocess calls, eval(), exec(), and os.system(). Consider adding pre-commit hooks or linting rules to prevent introduction of these patterns in future contributions.

findings.description: Excellent supply chain hygiene with SHA-pinned GitHub Actions and comprehensive dependency lockfile. All workflow actions use full commit SHA references.
findings.evidence: File .github/workflows/zizmor.yaml shows SHA-pinned actions: 'actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2' and 'zizmorcore/zizmor-action@71321a20a9ded102f6e9ce5718a2fcec2c4f70d8 # v0.5.2'. Additional security controls observed: 'persist-credentials: false' in checkout step, 'permissions: {}' (least privilege), and concurrency controls. Repository tree confirms presence of uv.lock (310,103 bytes) for deterministic Python dependency installation.
findings.recommendation: Continue current best practices for supply chain security. Maintain SHA pinning for all GitHub Actions. Keep uv.lock file updated with each dependency change. Consider implementing SLSA provenance generation for published packages to further enhance supply chain security.

findings.description: Advanced workflow security analysis integrated via zizmor tool, running on all YAML file changes with proper permissions and security configurations.
findings.evidence: File .github/workflows/zizmor.yaml configured with: on.pull_request paths: ['**/*.yaml?'], on.push branches: [main, stable] paths: ['**/*.yaml?'], permissions: {}, concurrency group set to prevent concurrent runs, and annotations: true for PR feedback. This represents proactive security monitoring of GitHub Actions workflows, which is an uncommon and advanced security practice.
findings.recommendation: Maintain the zizmor workflow configuration. Consider enabling 'advanced-security: true' if GitHub Advanced Security is available (requires GitHub Enterprise or public repository sponsorship). Monitor zizmor findings and address any security issues it identifies promptly.

findings.description: LICENSE.txt file is present at repository root with complete BSD 3-Clause License text and clear copyright attribution to Pallets (Copyright 2010).
findings.evidence:
File: LICENSE.txt (SHA: 9d227a0cc43c3268d15722b763bd94ad298645a1) found in repository tree. Content: 'Copyright 2010 Pallets

Redistribution and use in source and binary forms, with or without modification, are permitted provided that the following conditions are met: [full BSD-3-Clause text present]'
findings.recommendation: No action required. License file is properly formatted and complete.

findings.description: License is correctly declared in pyproject.toml using SPDX identifier (BSD-3-Clause) and explicitly references the LICENSE.txt file using modern PEP 639 metadata standards.
findings.evidence: pyproject.toml (SHA: 0cb10a5829a36edfbeedd4e3f99847eeb71d37b2) contains: [project] license = "BSD-3-Clause" and license-files = ["LICENSE.txt"]
findings.recommendation: No action required. Metadata is fully compliant with modern Python packaging standards.

findings.description: Source files do not contain copyright or license headers. While acceptable for BSD-3-Clause permissive licenses, adding SPDX headers would improve machine-readability for automated license scanning tools.
findings.evidence: File src/flask/__init__.py (SHA: 30dce6fd7d6b3b388c52a3c0b4893f0cff8e6007) contains no copyright header. Code search 'repo:pallets/flask path:src/flask extension:py "Copyright"' returned total_count: 0, incomplete_results: false
findings.recommendation: Optional: Consider adding minimal SPDX headers to source files (e.g., '# SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause') for improved compatibility with automated compliance tools like REUSE and ClearlyDefined.

findings.description: All core dependencies are from the Pallets ecosystem (blinker, click, itsdangerous, jinja2, markupsafe, werkzeug) and use compatible BSD-3-Clause or permissive MIT licenses. No license conflicts detected.
findings.evidence: pyproject.toml dependencies section lists: blinker>=1.9.0, click>=8.1.3, itsdangerous>=2.2.0, jinja2>=3.1.2, markupsafe>=2.1.1, werkzeug>=3.1.0. All are Pallets-maintained projects with BSD-3-Clause licenses.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Dependency licenses are fully compatible with Flask's BSD-3-Clause license.

findings.description: No NOTICE or COPYING files present. This is correct and compliant for BSD-3-Clause licensed projects, which only require LICENSE.txt inclusion in distributions.
findings.evidence: Repository tree (SHA: 2ac89889f4cc330eabd50f295dcef02828522c69) shows files: LICENSE.txt, CHANGES.rst, README.md present. No NOTICE or COPYING files found, which is appropriate for BSD-3-Clause.
findings.recommendation: No action required. NOTICE files are only required for Apache-2.0 and similar licenses, not BSD-3-Clause.

findings.description: License file is correctly included in source distribution configuration and project classifiers are appropriate for a BSD-licensed project.
findings.evidence: pyproject.toml contains license-files declaration and proper PyPI classifiers including 'Development Status :: 5 - Production/Stable' without license-specific classifiers (modern practice uses license field instead).
findings.recommendation: No action required. Distribution configuration follows current Python packaging best practices.
